text:
This land enters tapped.

text:
(When this Pawn enters, you may link a Pawn you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, replace it on the chess board with a Queen, then put two +1/+1 counters on her.

name: Annoy No More!
text: Whenever a chess piece you control or a Chess creature you control becomes the target of a spell, you may move it.
flavorText: "…And the black beast, vexed by the endless dance, sought to sever the threads that bound him to the Fae court, for a gentleman should not be made to tap his foot to such a wretched, unending tune."

name: Arena of Twisted Hunger
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, move a Pawn chess piece you control.
{T}: Add {R} or {G}.
flavorText: A place where dreams and nightmares devour each other, endlessly colliding in a ruthless survival of the strongest idea.

name: Art of War
text:
At the beginning of each combat, up to one target creature attacks or blocks this combat if able and up to one target creature can't attack or block this combat.
If a creature you control dealing combat damage causes a triggered ability of a permanent you control to trigger, that ability triggers an additional time.

name: Awake the Sleeping Giants
text: For each of X target permanents, create a token that's a copy of it, except it's a 4/4 artifact Chess Rook creature token in addition to its other types. For each token created this way, put a Rook chess piece on the chess board, each on an unoccupied square on your first and/or second row, linked to a different token created this way.

name: Back to Nature
text: Destroy all enchantments.
flavorText: There will always be those who presume to improve nature, or dare to befoul it. And nature will always have a response.

If at least one of your Rook chess pieces is missing, you may put a Rook chess piece onto one of your unoccupied starting Rook squares.
flavorText: "To rebuild is to admit that the world has broken us. To admit is to be whole again."

text: Whenever a Chess creature you control enters, instead of it replacing an existing chess piece, you may create a new chess piece that is a copy of it.
artist: James R. Eads and The Glitch
flavorText: This is where you become yourself.

name: Become Your True Self
text: Choose target non-King chess piece. It becomes a random non-King chess piece.
flavorText: “We all dream of impossible shapes. Fear nothing, for your subconscious is simply reminding you of what you have always been.”

text:

{4}, {T}: Investigate. (Create a Clue token. It's an artifact with "{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.")

name: Body Tender
text:
(When this Pawn enters, you may link a Pawn you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)

text:
Your opponents can't gain control of permanents and chess pieces you control.
Whenever a Knight you control jumps over a chess piece, draw two cards.
flavorText:
"Chetara is a world of the Puppeteers and their puppets. The puppets who have shattered their strings are freer than those who once controlled them."
—Nevilla, the Emerald-Clad

name: Breaker of Expectations
text:
(When this Bishop enters, you may link a Bishop you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)

text:
Choose one —
• Gain control of target Non-King, Non-Queen creature you don't control until end of turn. Untap that creature. It gains haste until end of turn.
• Move a Non-King, Non-Queen chess piece you don't control as though it was yours.

name: Change Places!
text: Switch the place of target chess piece you control with target chess piece you don't control.
flavorText:
"I'm tired of being a pawn," Alice remarked. "I think I'll try being a Queen."
"No, no! The rules are simple: you go there, and I go here, and neither of us knows why."

name: Chariot of Nobility
text:
Whenever you move your Queen, you may also move your King and vice versa.
{T}: Each player draws a card.
Crew 3
flavorText: Legend says that the Grand Puppeteer split their own love and hate between them. To preserve this fragile balance, a wondrous chariot was born that very day.

name: Chessling
text:
(When this Pawn enters, you may link a Pawn you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)

text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature has double strike.

name: Coat in Nightmares
text:
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature can't block and can't be blocked.
flavorText: To wear a skin not your own, to walk the shadows alone, to be everyone and no one at once—truly a dream made real.

name: Columination
text: Choose a column on the chess board. Destroy all non-King pieces on that column.
flavorText:

—The Tome of Birth and Death, First Revelation

name: Confuse Directions

flavorText: "If you're thinking only one step ahead, you're already falling behind." —Teysa

text: Simultaneously move all chess pieces you control one square forward, vertically, if the destination square is unoccupied by a King. If a chess piece would move into a square occupied by another piece, destroy the occupying piece.

name: Meditate
text: Draw four cards. You skip your next turn.
flavorText:
"Part of me believes that Barrin taught me meditation simply to shut me up."
—Ertai, wizard adept

name: Mesa Enchantress
text: Whenever you cast an enchantment spell, you may draw a card.
flavorText: To weary wanderers, trapped and lost amid Duskmourn's labyrinthine halls, her gifts are a welcome respite from meals of boiled ear-fungus, crickets, and the Floodpits' bizarre aquatic creatures.

name: Naturalize
text: Destroy target artifact or enchantment.
flavorText:

—Iveta, rooftop runner

As an additional cost to cast this spell, sacrifice a Queen.
Discard your hand, then draw seven cards.
flavorText:
She held the crown, her fingers trembling as centuries of lineage slipped from her grasp.
"A queen's duty is not to reign forever, but to know when her ending can save everything."

flavorText:
Serious at first,
Then everyone started grinning,
Rules took the afternoon off.

flavorText: "You hid behind countless puppets. Their deaths are yours to answer for."

Equip {3}
flavorText: "Some of us are born rich, and some of us are just born. Think of this as a bridge."

flavorText: "For one moment, I wanted to be strong—to be heard, to feel! I regret nothing! You puppets will fade to nothing while I be remembered as something!"

name: Quick Study
text: Draw two cards.
flavorText: At first, Johann was annoyed that the library had decided to reorganize itself. But he had to admit that storing the cookbooks in the cauldron made a certain kind of sense.

name: Raise Dead
text: Return target creature card from your graveyard to your hand.
flavorText: The earth cannot hold that which magic commands.

name: Rampant Growth
text: Search your library for a basic land card, put that card onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
flavorText: Nature grows solutions to its problems.

name: Reach for the Stars
text:
Surveil 2, then draw two cards. You lose 2 life.
If at least one of your Bishop chess pieces is missing, you may put a Bishop chess piece onto one of your unoccupied starting Bishop squares.
flavorText: "Chetara doesn't tolerate emptiness. Where one is gone, the other must take its place."

name: Reclamation Sage
text: When this creature enters, you may destroy target artifact or enchantment.
flavorText: "What was once formed by masons, shaped by smiths, or given life by mages, I will return to the embrace of the earth."

name: Regrowth
text: Return target card from your graveyard to your hand.
flavorText: While the root remains, the tree yet lives.

name: Resonance
text:
Resonance for permanents (This spell costs X less to cast, where X is the total mana value of permanents you control.)
Draw X cards.
flavorText: "Life, when lived to the fullest, teaches us that within each of us, there's the beauty of all of us."

name: Restock
text: Return two target cards from your graveyard to your hand. Exile Restock.
flavorText: What looked like a retreat was actually a replenishing.

name: Restoration Entity
text:
(When this Bishop enters, you may link a Bishop you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Lifelink
When this creature enters, return target enchantment card from your graveyard to your hand.

name: Reverie City
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, move a Pawn chess piece you control.
{T}: Add {R} or {W}.
flavorText: A place where dreams are cherished and loved, and where nightmares are shattered and released.

name: Rider's Bond
text:
Whenever you move one Knight, you may move another Knight you control.
Instead of moving Knights two squares vertically and one square horizontally, or two squares horizontally and one square vertically, you may do two and two.
flavorText: Chetara gives you every moment, all the time. Take the time to choose the ones that matter most.

name: Right-Hand of Greatness
text:
(When this Pawn enters, you may link a Pawn you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
{T}: Add {R}.
{5}{R}, {T}: Move a Knight you control.

name: Row Power
text: Choose a row on the chess board. Destroy all non-King pieces on that row.
flavorText:
"The Sovereign's hand weighs upon all, yet even the crown is bound by the turn of destiny; to touch is to tether, and to be touched is to endure the shadowy chains of fate."
—The Tome of Birth and Death, Second Revelation

name: Rugged Highlands
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Add {R} or {G}.

name: Sacrifice I'm Willing to Make
text: If a chess piece you control would be destroyed, you may sacrifice another chess piece of the same type instead.
flavorText:
"Do you weep for the rounding error in a calculation? Do you mourn the zero when it is crossed out to simplify a fraction? No! You celebrate the clarity!"
—Dev, the Madmatician

name: Savor the Moment
text: Take an extra turn after this one. Skip the untap step of that turn.
flavorText: The merrow took a moment for herself. She never gave it back.

name: Scare
text:
Choose one —
• Target creature you control gains menace until end of turn.
• Choose a chess piece you control. Move backwards each piece adjacent to it.

name: Scoured Barrens
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Add {W} or {B}.

name: Secret Passage
text:
Secret Passage enters the battlefield tapped.
{T}: Add {U} or {B}.
{4}, {T}: Investigate. (Create a Clue token. It's an artifact with "{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.")

name: Shatterstorm
text: Destroy all artifacts. They can't be regenerated.
flavorText: From a marvel of magical mechanization to a smoking stack of scrap in one easy step.

name: She of Gears Turning
text:
(When this Queen enters, you MUST link a Queen you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Vigilance
Creatures you control have "Ward—This creature's linked chess piece may move now."
Chess pieces you control have "Ward—Put a 'This creature can't be blocked.' counter on this creature."

name: She of Majestic Dread
text:
(When this Queen enters, you MUST link a Queen you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Menace
She of Majestic Dread's linked chess piece may jump over chess pieces. When she does, you may move each of those chess pieces to any adjacent unoccupied square diagonally, vertically or horizontally.

name: She That's Beyond Measure
text:
(When this Queen enters, you MUST link a Queen you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Reach, trample
Whenever She That's Beyond Measure's linked chess piece lands on a square, destroy all non-King chess pieces adjacent to her.

name: Shepherd of Emerald
text:
(When this Bishop enters, you may link a Bishop you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Vigilance
{T}: Add {G} for each chess piece this Bishop can see on the chess board.

name: Sing for the Forgotten
text: At the beginning of your upkeep, draw a card for each card type among cards in your graveyard, then return target card from your graveyard to your hand.
flavorText: Dreams and nightmares are ancient rivals, yet on quiet nights they sit together, grieving the same wound: how quickly mortals forget them by morning.

name: Singularity Convergence
text: Choose white squares or black squares. Destroy all non-King chess pieces on the chosen squares.
flavorText:
"Let it be written: on the Day Ordained, Radiance and Gloom divided the faithful, and one host was preserved while the other was unmade; not as mercy nor as wrath, but by decree, for the spared were condemned to remember, and remembrance became their curse."
—The Tome of Birth and Death, Third Revelation

name: Skewer
Sleeping Headstone
text:
(When this Rook enters, you may link a Rook you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Defender
As long as you control another Chess Rook creature, this creature can attack as though it didn't have defender.

name: Smash
text:
Destroy target artifact.
Draw a card.
flavorText: Ravnica's laws protect not its citizens but its industry. Aging equipment is destroyed rather than restored, to bring more money into the factories' coffers.

name: Sonneteer of the Departed
text:
(When this Bishop enters, you may link a Bishop you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Whenever a creature or a chess piece dies, you draw a card and you lose 1 life.

name: Sprawling Aqueduct
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, move a Pawn chess piece you control.
{T}: Add {G} or {U}.
flavorText: A place where dreams are not held in slumber, but set to work, turning the very gears of the world.

name: Stallion of Bad Omens
text:
(When this Knight enters, you may link a Knight you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Ward against white—Pay 4 life
Chess creatures linked to chess pieces on black squares get +2/+1.

name: Stallion of Good Omens
Starnheim Courser
text:
Flying
Artifact and enchantment spells you cast cost {1} less to cast.
flavorText: Every worthy warrior who arrives in Starnheim is gifted a mount befitting their heroism, a mighty pegasus raised and trained by the valkyries.

name: Strength of the Meek
text:
Flash
Enchant creature
Enchanted creature gets +1/+1 for each Pawn you control in the row that has the most Pawns you control or tied with the most.
flavorText: The choir is as beautiful as its smallest voice.

name: Study
text:
Study enters the battlefield tapped.
{T}: Add {W} or {U}.
{4}, {T}: Investigate. (Create a Clue token. It's an artifact with "{2}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw a card.")

name: Summon: The Bishop
text: Put up to one Bishop on the chess board onto one of your unoccupied starting Bishop squares.
flavorText: Born into poverty in a family devoted to Phenax on Theros, he wanders across all of Chetara in search of a Puppeteer worthy of his devotion—one whose will might one day reflect his own.

name: Summon: The Knight
text: Put up to one Knight on the chess board onto one of your unoccupied starting Knight squares.
flavorText: Born under the ever-watchful eye of the Mother of Machines on New Phyrexia, he wanders across all of Chetara in search of a Puppeteer worthy of perfection—made compleat in her image.

name: Summon: The Pawn
text: Put up to one Pawn on the chess board onto one of your unoccupied starting Pawn squares.
flavorText: Born to an angel and a demon on Innistrad, she wanders across all of Chetara in search of a Puppeteer worthy of her divided soul—redeemable, but broken.

name: Summon: The Queen
text: Put up to one Queen on the chess board onto your unoccupied starting Queen square.
flavorText: Born to a wealthy Faefolk family on Eldraine, she wanders across all of Chetara in search of a mischievous Puppeteer worthy of a shared adventure—one that begins with running away together.

name: Summon: The Rook
text: Put up to one Rook on the chess board onto one of your unoccupied starting Rook squares.
flavorText: Born at the ever-shifting soils of Zendikar, it wanders across all of Chetara in search of a Puppeteer worthy of its awakening power—ever shifting, yet unyielding.

name: Summon: The Twins
text: Put up to two Pawns on the chess board onto two unoccupied starting Pawn squares.
flavorText: Born beneath the twin suns of Amonkhet, they wander across all of Chetara in search of a Puppeteer worthy of wielding both merciful Warmth and searing Judgment—and will yield to neither.

name: Swift Recognition
text:
Flash
Spells you cast have flash.
Whenever you cast a spell during an opponent's turn, you may draw a card.
flavorText: Those who learn from the fox's cunning quickly realize how to quickly realize.

name: Swiftwater Cliffs
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Add {U} or {R}.

name: Teachings of the Darkest Night
text:
Whenever a Bishop creature you control enters or dies, search your library for a non-Bishop card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
{B}, Sacrifice three Pawns: Return target Bishop creature card from your graveyard to the battlefield with a finality counter on it. Activate only as a sorcery.

name: Tell Your Stories
text:
Whenever an enchantment you control enters, you draw a card and you gain 2 life.
At the beginning of your upkeep, search your library for an enchantment card, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle.
flavorText: Chetara is a plane of all that the mind can imagine, where even the speechless may find their voice.

name: Temporal Manipulation
text: Take an extra turn after this one.
flavorText: Doing something at the last minute isn't so bad when you can make that minute last.

name: That's Tomorrow's Problem
text: The owner of target nonland permanent or target spell puts it into their library second from the top or on the bottom.
flavorText: "The concept of time does not exist here, and yet, paradoxically, we have all the time in the world…"

name: The Blessed Shell Bless
text: Target creature you control gets +2/+2 until end of turn. If you control a Bishop Chess creature, it also gains lifelink, hexproof and indestructible until end of turn.
flavorText: "I am merely a conduit of the Absolute Puppeteer. By Their decree, you may become unbreakable."

name: The Door Between All Doors
text: At the beginning of your chess phase, return a chess piece you're missing to its possible starting position, if that position is unoccupied. If you control all sixteen chess pieces, you win the game.
flavorText:
"Welcome back, now let's turn some tables!"
—Tarian, the Mischievous Judge

name: The Emerald King
text:
(When this King enters, you MUST link a King you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Permanents you control have indestructible.
Chess pieces you control can't be destroyed, and chess pieces you don't control can't be moved into their squares.

name: The Indomitable One
text:
(When this Queen enters, you MUST link a Queen you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Vigilance
Whenever a chess piece moves outside of the chess phase, you may stop it on any unoccupied square it passes through.

name: The Killer Instinct
text:
(When this Queen enters, you MUST link a Queen you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Deathtouch
At the beginning of your chess phase, destroy all non-King chess pieces adjacent to The Killer Instinct's linked chess piece.

name: The Marble King
text:
(When this King enters, you MUST link a King you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
When The Marble King enters, choose an emblem —
• You can only lose the game by losing the chess game.
• You can only lose the game by losing the Magic: The Gathering game.

name: The Moon King
text:
(When this King enters, you MUST link a King you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
When The Moon King enters, each player chooses half of their chess pieces, rounded up, then sacrifices the rest. They also do so to their creatures.
At the beginning of your end step, return all permanents you sacrificed to the battlefield and all chess pieces you sacrificed to the chess board, each on their starting position, and only if it's unoccupied.

name: The Nyx King
text:
(When this King enters, you MUST link a King you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Permanents you control have hexproof.
When The Nyx King enters, draw any number of cards. You get an emblem with "You have no maximum hand size."

name: The Obsidian King
text:
(When this King enters, you MUST link a King you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
When The Obsidian King enters, exile all non-King permanents. Then, each player's life total becomes 1 and if it's your turn, end the turn.

name: The Pretender King
text:
(When this Pawn enters, you may link a Pawn you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
The Pretender King is a King as long as it's on the battlefield. (It has its move set and it can be checkmated. You don't lose the game if you have at least one king.)

name: Their Terrifying Presence
text:
Chess Rook creatures you control can't be blocked.
Rook chess pieces you control can jump over chess pieces.
Whenever a chess piece you control jumps over a chess piece and whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a player, you may draw a card. If you do, discard a card.

name: Thornwood Falls
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Add {G} or {U}.

name: Thoughtseize
text: Target player reveals their hand. You choose a nonland card from it. That player discards that card. You lose 2 life.
flavorText: "Any dream is a robust harvest. Still, I prefer the timeworn dreams, heavy with import, that haunt the obsessive mind."

name: Through Infinite Doors
text: Exile target creature or chess piece you control. Return it under its owner's control at the beginning of the next end step. (Chess pieces return to the chess board at their starting position, and only if it's unoccupied.)

name: Towering Smasher
text:
(When this Rook enters, you may link a Rook you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves the battlefield, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. When this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Reach, trample
Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, destroy target artifact or land that player controls.

name: Tranquil Cove
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Add {W} or {U}.

name: Vile Arch-Darknoid
text:
(When this Bishop enters, you may link a Bishop you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
At the beginning of your upkeep, sacrifice a chess piece.

name: Violence Over Reason
text:
Bishops, Rooks and Queens you control may jump over one chess piece you don't control as though they were Knights.
Whenever a chess piece you control jumps over a non-King chess piece, destroy it.
flavorText: Beware the praying man when the books are closed.

name: Viridian Revel
text: Whenever an artifact is put into an opponent's graveyard from the battlefield, you may draw a card.
flavorText:
"Let them have their revel. Soon those who won't submit will be turned. Those who won't be turned will die."
—Vorinclex, Voice of Hunger

name: Voidglass Castellan
text:
(When this Rook enters, you may link a Rook you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
Deathtouch
When this creature dies, if it had no counters on it, return it to the battlefield tapped under its owner's control with a stun counter on it.

name: Watershell Ancient
text:
(When this Rook enters, you may link a Rook you control on the chess board with it. When this leaves, that chess piece also leaves and vice versa. Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, you may also move the linked chess piece on the chess board.)
When this creature enters, draw two cards.
Vigilance

name: Where Ancients Sing
text:
Whenever you cast a Rook spell, draw two cards.
Rook chess pieces you control may also move diagonally.
flavorText: Legend tells that they do not move—reality simply rearranges itself around them.

name: Wind-Scarred Crag
text:
This land enters tapped.
When this land enters, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Add {R} or {W}.

name: Worst Fears
text: You control target player during that player's next turn. Exile Worst Fears. (You see all cards that player could see and make all decisions for the player.)
flavorText: Elspeth feared that her trespass into Nyx would not go unanswered.

name: Zombify
text: Return target creature card from your graveyard to the battlefield.
flavorText:
"The first birth celebrates life. The second birth mocks it."
—Mystic elder

name: Zugzwang
text: Sacrifice any number of non-King chess pieces. Draw a card and add {B} or {R} for each chess piece sacrificed this way.
flavorText:
"As the moons of my former home went dark, I saw thy names bleed for me. The clock has stopped. The throne is a tomb. Everything is exactly where I left it."
—Voraq, The Laughing Void
